key_insight: SciVerseGym turns crystal discovery into a Gymnasium-style sequential decision problem, letting agents edit structures and get immediate physics-based feedback.
engineering_relevance: It provides a reusable, open testbed for RL, Bayesian optimization, and other closed-loop search methods in materials design without rebuilding the atomistic workflow each time.
method: Supports local and global crystal actions: substitution, lattice perturbation, atomic displacement, vacancy creation, and atom insertion.
Uses configurable structure pools, chemical spaces, atomistic or graph observations, and custom rewards.
Evaluates each step with a machine-learned interatomic potential or any ASE-compatible calculator, with optional relaxation and stability/phonon diagnostics.
evidence_signal: The abstract reports a Gymnasium-compatible environment with standard step outputs, configurable evaluators, and multiple diagnostics for candidate structures, but does not provide benchmark performance numbers.
watch_item: Performance and reliability will depend on the quality of the chosen potential, reward design, and whether the relaxation/evaluation pipeline is consistent across chemical spaces.
paper_title: SVGym (SciVerseGym): An Environment for Reinforcement Learning and Bayesian Optimization in Crystal Discovery
